name: MetaGPT
description: Multi-agent framework that assigns AI roles (product manager, architect, project manager, engineer) to collaborate on full software deliverables from a single natural-language requirement.

description.value: Multi-agent SOP framework that simulates a software company by assigning LLM roles (PM, architect, project manager, engineer) to collaborate end-to-end from a one-line requirement to a generated repo.

creator.comment: Originally published under the geekan/MetaGPT repo by Alexander Wu; later moved to the FoundationAgents GitHub org. DeepWisdom is the affiliated company.

first-release.comment: MetaGPT paper and initial public release in 2023; v0.1 tagged that year.

latest-release.comment: v0.8.2 published 2025-03-09 (verified via gh API). No tagged release since then; main branch has continued to receive merged PRs.

pricing-model.comment: Framework itself is free OSS. DeepWisdom operates a separate commercial product (MGX/MetaGPT X) at mgx.dev, but it is not the OSS workflow being evaluated here.

covered-phases.comment: SOP runs PM (PRD/spec) → Architect (system design) → Project Manager (task breakdown) → Engineer (code). Outputs include user stories, requirements, data structures, APIs, and documents.

methodology-style.comment: Distinct LLM-driven roles per phase (true multi-agent), built around a fixed SOP (process-centric), starting from a written specification (spec-driven).

opinionated.comment: Prescribes a fixed SOP with role pipeline; deviation requires writing custom Roles/Actions in code.

ceremony-level.comment: Default workflow produces multiple distinct artifacts (PRD, design, task list, code) across formal role handoffs.

mechanization-level.comment: Single CLI command (e.g. `metagpt "write a cli blackjack game"`) runs the entire SOP end-to-end.

hitl-checkpoints.comment: Default workflow runs autonomously through phases; no built-in per-phase approval gates documented.

autonomous-e2e.comment: A single CLI invocation produces a generated repo in ./workspace without further human input.

autonomy-level.comment: Default configuration runs all phases without human approval; iteration count is bounded by --n-round (default 5).

rollback-support.comment: v0.6.0 introduced breakpoint recovery / serialization for resuming paused runs, but no explicit revert/undo of generated artifacts.

supported-agents.comment: MetaGPT is its own agent runtime, not a workflow layered on top of an existing coding agent. Empty per RESEARCH.md guideline.

editor-integrations.comment: Primary interface is a Python CLI / library; no first-party editor extensions.

model-agnostic.comment: Supports OpenAI, Azure, Ollama, Groq, Anthropic Claude, Gemini, QianFan, DashScope, Yi, and other providers via configurable LLMType.

mcp-support.comment: No MCP references found in the repo (gh code search returned 0 results) or in any release notes through v0.8.2 (2025-03).

artifact-formats.comment: PRDs, designs, and user-facing docs are produced as markdown. Some intermediate task representations are JSON, but markdown is the primary user-readable artifact format.

artifacts-covered.comment: README lists user stories, competitive analysis, requirements, data structures, APIs, and documents as outputs.

project-layout.value: Generated outputs are written to ./workspace/<project-name>/, containing the PRD, system design, task list, and generated source code.
project-layout.comment: README explicitly states 'this will create a repo in ./workspace'. The --project-name flag controls the subdirectory name.

artifacts-in-git.comment: Outputs go to ./workspace/ as a generated repo rather than being interleaved with an existing project's git history; users may commit them but it is not the framework's design.

language-agnostic.comment: Generated code language is driven by the requirement; defaults and examples lean Python but other languages can be requested.

enforcement-mechanisms.comment: Process discipline is enforced through SOP prompts and role/message contracts in code; no built-in CI checks, hooks, or test gates.

strict-phase-ordering.comment: The SOP fixes role/phase ordering (PM → Architect → PjM → Engineer); changing it requires editing the team composition in code.

artifact-validation.comment: No documented schema validation of produced artifacts; outputs are LLM-generated markdown/code.

memory-implementation.comment: Per-agent Memory plus shared Environment messages within a run. v0.6.0 added serialization for breakpoint recovery, but cross-session memory is not the default model.

persistent-memory.comment: Serialization/breakpoint recovery exists for resuming an interrupted run, but there is no general persistent memory shared across separate user sessions.

project-context-files.comment: Does not use or produce CLAUDE.md / AGENTS.md style project-scoped context files; configuration lives in YAML config files instead.

cost-tracking.comment: CLI exposes --investment as a max-budget cap (default $3) handled by an internal cost manager that tracks token spend per run.

run-history.comment: No built-in structured run history or trace database; only standard logs and the generated workspace artifacts.

telemetry-opt.comment: No documented telemetry collection by the OSS framework itself; could not confirm one way or the other from public docs.

custom-templates.comment: Users define custom Action subclasses with their own prompt templates and run() logic.

hooks.comment: No documented pre/post-phase hook system. The closest analog is overriding _act() inside a custom Role, which is application code rather than a lifecycle hook.

sub-agents.comment: Users can define custom Roles (with profile/name/actions) and assemble them into a Team — this is the canonical extension point.

marketplace.comment: No public marketplace or shared-templates registry; community examples live in the repo's examples/ directory.

install-mechanism.comment: Primary install is `pip install --upgrade metagpt`; a Dockerfile is also provided in the repo.

setup-time.comment: pip install plus an LLM API key in a YAML config; the quickstart fits comfortably under 5 minutes if an API key is on hand.

self-hosted.comment: Runs entirely as a local Python process; LLM API calls are the only external dependency.

requires-account.comment: No DeepWisdom/MetaGPT account is required. An LLM provider API key is needed (e.g., OpenAI), but that is a separate concern from the framework itself.

git-worktree-support.comment: No documented git worktree integration. Outputs are written to ./workspace/ regardless of git state.

documentation-quality.comment: docs.deepwisdom.ai provides English and Chinese docs covering quickstart, concepts, agent tutorials, and use cases. Coverage is broad but some sections (workspace layout, telemetry, lifecycle) are thin.

github-stars.comment: 67,317 stars at 2026-04-21 (per gh API). One of the highest-starred multi-agent frameworks.

active-maintenance.comment: Most recent commit on main is 2026-01-21 (Windows Terminal support PR). That sits exactly at the 90-day boundary from the 2026-04-21 research date — borderline. No tagged release since v0.8.2 (2025-03).

example-projects.comment: Repo includes an examples/ directory with multiple worked scenarios; quickstart docs include several end-to-end demos.

community-channel.comment: Official Discord server linked from the README and docs. No other first-party channel observed.
